notes: Gravity for the BROKEN GLASS FRACTAL scenario. Dream-logic permitted in the Priestess only — the shard catches angles it has not been hit by; things shift on shelves without being moved; the clock is wrong; the dash posts. The bungalow is becoming, briefly, a set. Pressing DOUBT harder than STAGNATION; the haunting tightens around the editing desk.

deck.title: the shard catches an angle
deck.flavor: from a window you have not, in this season of the year, seen the light come through. you note the angle. you check the window. the angle is correct. the room is wrong.

deck.title: the laptop drops a frame
deck.flavor: in the anya clip. one frame, mid-breath. you scrub back. the frame is, this pass, there. you scrub forward. it is not, this pass, there.

deck.title: the clock is two minutes off
deck.flavor: the kitchen wall clock. you have, this morning, set it to the phone. the phone now reads 9:11. the wall reads 9:09.

deck.title: the latch nudges itself
deck.flavor: the storage closet door, ajar by half an inch. you have not, since november, opened it. you walk past. the latch clicks closed behind you.

deck.title: the dash posts
deck.flavor: under XVII. a second comment in the same year. you have not, in four years, seen the handle post twice on the same episode. you do not, this turn, refresh the page.

deck.title: the kettle whistles at the wrong pitch
deck.flavor: you did not, this turn, put it on. the burner is cold. the whistle is, you note, a quarter-tone flat of the way the listing burner usually pulls it.

deck.title: a piece of basil on the editing desk
deck.flavor: a single leaf. yellow. you have not, this turn, been to the kitchen. the leaf is, you note, from the dying basil. the leaf is, today, on the laptop.

deck.title: anya's clip frame-jumps
deck.flavor: the cursor stays where you left it. the picture in the clip moves on its own. anya finishes the sentence. the sentence is the sentence. you did not, this turn, click play.

deck.title: the welcome mat has shifted six inches
deck.flavor: from the porch position you, in '23, placed it in. you note. you do not, this turn, move it back.

deck.title: the picture in the dresser is, today, not face-down
deck.flavor: you walk past the bedroom door. the picture is up. you have, since november, kept it down. you walk back to the editing desk. you do not, this turn, go in.

deck.title: the printer queues a job
deck.flavor: in the storage closet. you did not, this turn, send anything to print. you walk past. the printer makes the small chunking noise the printer makes when it is, in fact, printing.

deck.title: the shard is on the fifth shelf
deck.flavor: you put it on the third. it is on the fifth. you have not, today, moved it. you do not, this turn, move it back.

deck.title: the cicadas stop, briefly
deck.flavor: outside. for the count of eleven. you have, in two years, never timed it. tonight you do. eleven seconds. then they resume.

deck.title: you find a box you did not pack
deck.flavor: in the corner of the living room. taped. labeled in your sharpie. KEEP. you have not, in any of the six weeks of packing, taped a box and written that word.

deck.title: the bungalow registers, for half a second, as a set
deck.flavor: from the kitchen doorway. you see the living room the way you see a take through the camera. the room reads as a room someone has dressed.
